title: Hoff (Colby Hoff's son)
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Otheruses|Hoff}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Hoff-SOTETC.png]] |name=Hoff |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]],Hoff is [[Death/Legends|killed]] while attacking [[Dark Prince|Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]] in the [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Shadows of the Empire'' novel]], the events of which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]]. [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]][[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' novel]] |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]{{Topps|set=1996 Topps Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|cardname=Xizor's Dirty Handiwork|link=https://www.rebelscum.com/photo.asp?image=/sote/soteTC007.jpg}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height=Taller than 1.83 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]The [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Shadows of the Empire'' novel]] describes Hoff as being "much larger than [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]]," whom ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' establishes is 1.83 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall. |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents=[[Colby Hoff]] |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|You killed my father. Have you forgotten Colby Hoff?|Hoff, to Prince Xizor|}} '''Hoff''' was the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|son]] of [[Colby Hoff]], a former business competitor of [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] [[Underlord|crime lord]] [[Dark Prince|Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]]. Colby fell into ruin and then committed [[Death/Legends|suicide]] after failing to outsmart his rival, leading the younger Hoff to blame Xizor for his [[Parent/Legends|father's]] murder. In retaliation and with the help of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], who also sought Xizor's destruction, Hoff ambushed the Black Sun leader inside a protected corridor on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Although larger and more powerfully built, the enraged Hoff could not kill Xizor, who easily defeated the would-be [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] and snapped his neck. Xizor suspected Vader's hand in the ambush but never learned who had granted Hoff access to the secure corridor. ==Biography== {{Quote|You are mistaken, Hoff. Your father committed suicide, as I recall. Stuck a blaster in his mouth and blew the back of his head off, didn't he? Very messy.''"
"''You ruined him!''"
"''We were ''business'' competitors. He gambled that he was smarter than I. A foolish mistake.|Hoff and Prince Xizor|}} Hoff was the [[Gender/Legends|son]] of the businessman [[Colby Hoff]], once a pesky rival''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{C|"Hoff"}} of [[Dark Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]], the [[Falleen/Legends|Falleen]] [[Underlord]] of the [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] crime syndicate. After a failed bid to outsmart Xizor left him ruined, Colby committed [[Death/Legends|suicide]] by firing a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] into his [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]].{{CSWECite|II|48|Hoff, Colby}} The younger Hoff blamed Xizor for what he considered the murder of his [[Parent/Legends|father]] and sought vengeance against the Black Sun leader. [[File:HoffGetsHassled-SOTETC.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Xizor snaps Hoff's neck.]] In [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] supplied Hoff with an opportunity to retaliate, as the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] was himself presently locked in a power struggle with Xizor and wished to see him eliminated. On Vader's orders, a checkpoint guard allowed Hoff access to a secure corridor deep inside the core of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], where Hoff soon ambushed Xizor and four accompanying [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]] as they were approaching. Blaster in [[Limb/Legends|hand]], Hoff immediately opened fire on Xizor from a distance and dropped one of the bodyguards with a [[Laser/Legends|bolt]] to the chest, but a second guard scored a direct hit on Hoff's weapon, knocking it from his grip. With a scream, Hoff then charged at Xizor barehanded. At close quarters, Hoff's enraged attack was no match for Xizor's [[Unarmed combat/Legends|martial-arts]] prowess. Ordering his three remaining guards to stand down, Xizor calmly parried Hoff's repeated advances, stunning him with a kick to the stomach; knocking him to the ground with a hammerfist to the head; and then disabling him by kicking and breaking his left knee joint. As the wounded Hoff condemned Xizor for his father's demise, the Dark Prince took hold of the man's head and snapped his neck, declaring that the younger Hoff's death would likewise be judged a suicide for being foolish enough to attack him. With that, Xizor ordered his guards to clean up Hoff's body and to inform the proper authorities of his fate. A life-size [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] [[Holorecorder/Legends|recording]] of Hoff's attack on Xizor later made its way before [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader, the latter displeased that his scheme to kill his rival had failed. Vader had seen to it that the guard who had let Hoff into the protected corridor was killed, thereby eliminating the only person with knowledge of the Dark Lord's involvement. Although Xizor suspected Vader, he was never able to determine who had granted Hoff access. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|I'm going to kill you!''"
"''I think not.|Hoff's last words before Xizor snaps his neck|}} Hoff was a large [[Human/Legends|Human]], standing taller than 1.83 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] and built like a heavy-[[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] weight lifter. He was bigger than Xizor's bodyguards and much larger than the Falleen himself, and his roundhouse punch was powerful enough to shatter [[Bone/Legends|bone]]. Desperate to enact retribution against the man whom he blamed for the murder of his father, Hoff assaulted Xizor in a crazed fury, charging the Black Sun leader's three armed guards without a weapon. Yet Hoff's wild attacks utterly failed against Xizor, who easily subdued his younger assailant with practiced fighting techniques while hardly breaking a sweat. Hoff had [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and light skin. ==Equipment== Hoff wore [[Coruscant Guard armor]] covering his chest, arms, and shoulders during his attack on Prince Xizor.{{Topps|set=1996 Topps Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|cardname=Xizor's Dirty Handiwork|link=https://www.rebelscum.com/photo.asp?image=/sote/soteTC007.jpg}} depicts Hoff as wearing [[Color/Legends|red]] body [[Armor/Legends|armor]] distinctly similar to the [[Coruscant Guard armor|armor]] worn by [[Coruscant Guard/Legends|Coruscant Guards]] in the trading card {{Topps|set=1996 Topps Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|cardname=Battle Over Coruscant: Part I|link=https://www.rebelscum.com/photo.asp?image=/sote/soteTC066.jpg}}. The ''[[Shadows of the Empire Sourcebook]]'' identifies the [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] from the aforementioned card image as [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] Guards. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:XizorHoff-ArtofBrothersHildebrandt.png|thumb|right|180px|Original sketch drawing of Xizor killing Hoff for the ''Shadows of the Empire'' trading card set]] In [[April]] [[1996]],{{InsiderCite|28|Lucasfilm's Latest}} Hoff appeared in the novel [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Shadows of the Empire'']], written by [[Steve Perry]]. Hoff's attack on Xizor is meant to demonstrate the Black Sun leader's considerable ability to defend himself against deadly attack as he vies with Darth Vader for the Emperor's favor. In [[September]] of that year,{{GalaxyCite|8|Around the Galaxy}} [[:File:SOTE-SWGM8.png|promotional spread]] Artists [[Greg Hildebrandt|Greg]] and [[Tim Hildebrandt]] created the Hoff character illustration as part of the [[Topps]] [[1996 Topps Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|''Shadows of the Empire'' trading card set]]. The set's "Xizor's Dirty Handiwork" card captures the moment as Xizor snaps Hoff's neck. The ''Shadows of the Empire'' novel describes the protected corridor where Hoff ambushes Xizor as a type of public walkway in the core of [[Galactic City/Legends|Imperial City]] where the elite [[Coruscanti/Legends|citizenry]] could travel without being disturbed. However, the "Xizor's Dirty Handiwork" card from the ''Shadows of the Empire'' trading card set states that Hoff attacks Xizor in a corridor belonging to [[Xizor's Palace]]. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' novel]] {{1st|in book}} *[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (junior novelization)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' junior novelization]] {{Hologram}} ==Sources== *''[[The Secrets of Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]'' {{Imo}} *{{Topps|set=1996 Topps Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|cardname=Xizor's Dirty Handiwork|link=https://www.rebelscum.com/photo.asp?image=/sote/soteTC007.jpg}} {{1stp}} *''[[Star Wars: The Art of the Brothers Hildebrandt]]'' {{Po}} *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Hoff }} [[Category:Assassins]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]]
